reasoning: As of March 1, 2026, "Hamnet" has an extremely low chance of winning Best Picture. While it was definitively a contender early on—scoring 8 Oscar nominations and a Golden Globe for Best Motion Picture (Drama)—it has missed crucial precursors. Best Picture winners almost invariably require an Academy Award nomination for Film Editing, which "Hamnet" failed to secure. More importantly, it lost the top prizes at both the BAFTAs (Feb 22) and the Producers Guild Awards (Feb 28 / Mar 1) to "One Battle After Another," which is now the overwhelming frontrunner. Betting markets reflect this reality: "Hamnet" plummeted to just a 4% chance on the Kalshi market even prior to losing these key industry precursors. Historical precedent tells us that a film failing to win the BAFTA, the PGA, and missing a Film Editing nomination has virtually a 0–2% probability of pulling an upset. I am placing the probability at the minimum allowable lower bound of 0.05 per the prompt instructions (effectively representing ~1-2%).
